project_description: Complete overhaul of ForgeOS — an AI-powered web app builder with a cockpit UI, workspace runner, and GitHub-to-Render deploy chain — replacing a broken multi-agent pipeline with a single Claude-powered builder, persistent memory brain, and clean agent boundaries.

shipped: Fixed 'Invalid Date' bug — published_at BIGINT from Neon returned as string, fixed with Number(row.published_at)
Fixed 'Workspace files not found' on restore — rebuildWorkspaceFromSnapshot now checks both executor.output.files and builder.output.files
Fixed Global Secrets not passing to published app child processes — getMergedEnv now calls getSecretsAsObject()
Migrated ForgeOS to Render persistent disk at /data — all workspace and published app paths now use DATA_DIR env var
Fixed all hardcoded __dirname-relative data paths across workspace/manager.js, publish/manager.js, projects/manager.js, runtime-backup.js, pipeline/runner.js, builder.js, index.js
Built email auth gate — server/auth/gate.js with HMAC-signed cookie session and ALLOWED_EMAILS whitelist
Built Render API wrapper — server/publish/render-api.js with createService, updateServiceEnv, redeployService, getServiceStatus, deleteService, listServices
Built pushToAppBranch in server/publish/github.js — pushes app files to dedicated apps/<slug> branch with standalone empty parent commit
Added render_service_id and render_url columns to published_apps table
decisions: Published apps deploy as independent Render Web Services, not child processes
Each app gets its own branch apps/<slug> in BrianBMorgan/ForgeOS repo
Render auto-deploys from that branch on push
DATA_DIR=/data on Render for all persistent data
ALLOWED_EMAILS env var controls ForgeOS access
Global Secrets Vault passes secrets to published app Render services via updateServiceEnv

session_log.session_title: Render Publish Pipeline, Wildcard Subdomains, Assets
shipped: Rewrote _doPublish to use Render API — pushes to GitHub branch, creates/redeploys Render service, passes env vars
Fixed render-api.js exports — pushProjectToGitHub was incorrectly included
Fixed envSpecificDetails requirement in Render API createService call
Fixed getSecretsAsObject lazy DB init — secrets now pass automatically to every published Render service
Fixed handle existing Render service on republish — graceful fallback when service name already in use
Replaced child-process restore with Render service restore in _restoreOne
Added renderUrl to getPublishedApp response and Publish tab UI
Major dead code scrub of publish/manager.js
Wildcard *.forge-os.ai DNS and TLS via Namecheap + Render — all three CNAME records added
Built wildcard subdomain proxy middleware in index.js
Fixed content encoding bug in subdomain proxy — accept-encoding: identity prevents ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED
Updated Publish tab UI to show subdomain URL as primary public URL
Added slug editor to Publish tab
Built renameSlug in publish/manager.js
Stage 1 tech stack unbans — react CDN, dotenv, jsonwebtoken, svelte CDN removed from banned list
Built forge_assets table in Neon (global scope, no project_id)
Built server/assets/manager.js — saveAsset, getAsset, listAssets, deleteAsset, getAssetsContext
Built assets API routes in index.js — GET/POST/DELETE with busboy multipart handling
Built global Assets tab in cockpit sidebar with drag-and-drop upload, file list, Copy URL, Delete
Injected AVAILABLE GLOBAL ASSETS block into builder prompt via builder.js
decisions: Assets stored in Neon DB as base64 (binary) or UTF-8 text (CSV/JSON/text)
Assets are global scope — no project_id, accessible by all projects
Asset access URL pattern: /api/assets/:filename
Wildcard subdomain proxy uses fetch with accept-encoding: identity to avoid compression issues
Slug rename creates new Render service and deletes old — no in-place rename
BASE_DOMAIN=forge-os.ai env var controls subdomain proxy domain
Session log protocol established — appended to Forge_additional_context_pack.json at end of each session

session_log.session_title: Dead Code Cleanup, Bug Fixes, and VS Code Workflow Migration
shipped: Removed self-require from server/pipeline/agents.js — line 12 had agents.js requiring itself, causing circular dependency warnings on startup
Fixed subdomain proxy body forwarding in server/index.js — changed body: req to body: JSON.stringify(req.body) and added content-type header
Fixed NEON_DATABASE_URL vs DATABASE_URL mismatch — published apps must use process.env.NEON_DATABASE_URL
Fixed Render API createService plan — changed plan: 'starter' to plan: 'standard' in render-api.js
Wired assets context injection into builder.js — getAssetsContext() call injected into systemPrompt before build
Restored all prompt constants to agents.js — FORGE_VOICE, EXECUTOR_INSTRUCTIONS, EXECUTOR_ITERATE_INSTRUCTIONS, EXECUTOR_FIX_INSTRUCTIONS, AUDITOR_INSTRUCTIONS, CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS
Migrated dev workflow from Replit to VS Code + GitHub
decisions: Replit is sunset as the dev environment — VS Code is now the sole development environment
All changes pushed directly from VS Code to GitHub, Render auto-deploys from main
Claude can push directly to GitHub repo via PAT when provided — eliminates manual file copy workflow
Subdomain proxy must serialize req.body as JSON string, not pipe req stream directly

session_log.session_title: Assets End-to-End Fix, Stage 2 Tech Stack Unbans, Template Literal Cleanup
shipped: Fixed getAssetsContext() return type — was returning array of objects (rendered as [object Object]), now returns formatted string
Added GLOBAL ASSETS usage section to BUILDER_SYSTEM_PROMPT — builder now knows URL pattern, fetch pattern, and when to use client-side vs server-side fetch
Added /api/assets/* passthrough to preview proxy — /preview/:runId middleware now short-circuits asset requests to ForgeOS root handler instead of forwarding to workspace app
Fixed CSV Grid Viewer end-to-end — asset pipeline fully working, 20-row Intel attendee dataset rendered correctly
Stage 2 tech stack unbans — react, react-dom, vue, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react removed from banned list
Added Mode A / Mode B runtime structure to BUILDER_SYSTEM_PROMPT — Mode A is plain Node (default), Mode B is Vite+React (opt-in)
Added vite.config.js pattern, correct PORT binding, and Mode B checklist items to builder prompt
Fixed three syntax errors in builder.js and agents.js caused by PR #33 (Claude Code branch) — inline backtick-wrapped paths inside template literals broke Node parser on Render
Closed all three claude/ branches — access-json-context-pack, fix-asset-url-instructions, review-forgeos-github-push — all absorbed or harmless
Re-enabled Render auto-deploy — had been disabled, re-enabled via Render dashboard
Added brain memory entries for assets proxy passthrough pattern
decisions: Preview proxy must pass /api/assets/* requests through to ForgeOS root handler — workspace apps cannot serve these routes
Generated apps must fetch global assets client-side using root-relative /api/assets/FILENAME — not server-side via localhost
Stage 2 unbans: react, react-dom, vue, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react approved. angular, nuxt, svelte, webpack, esbuild, parcel, rollup remain banned
Mode B (Vite+React) is opt-in only — builder defaults to Mode A (plain Node) unless React/Vue explicitly requested
Never write inline backtick-wrapped content inside JS template literal strings — use plain text or escaped backticks instead
Claude Code PRs must not be merged without review — PR #33 introduced syntax errors in two files

session_log.session_title: Visual Editor (Phases 1-3), Dead Code Cleanup, Version History, Custom Domains
shipped: Mode B preview notice — Vite apps show 'Go to Publish' notice instead of broken iframe in cockpit
BYO custom domain support — render-api.js: addCustomDomain, listCustomDomains, removeCustomDomain; publish/manager.js: setCustomDomain, deleteCustomDomain; Publish tab UI for custom domain management
Fixed asset upload crash — binary files now always stored and served as base64, never raw UTF-8

Version history with git tags and rollback — tagAndDeleteAppBranch, listVersionTags, restoreFromTag in github.js; listVersions, rollbackToVersion in publish/manager.js; Version History section in Publish tab
Dead code cleanup — removed dead agent prompt imports from runner.js
Deleted server/pipeline/agents.js — CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS moved inline to server/chat/manager.js
Deleted server/pipeline/schemas.js — ExecutorSchema/AuditorSchema unused since single-builder migration
Removed STAGES constant and pending-init loop from runner.js — stages written directly by builder
Phase 2: Edit tab — split-pane file editor (file tree + textarea) with live preview iframe; save writes to disk, restarts workspace, refreshes preview; dirty state tracking, Revert button

Phase 1: Inspect Mode — dormant inspector script injected into every preview HTML response via rewriteHtmlForProxy; Inspect toggle button in Live Preview header; blue overlay on hover; click captures selector + outerHTML + textContent; selection badge with Use in chat / dismiss

Phase 3: Inspect-to-Edit bridge — useSelection fires background file search (GET /api/runs/:id/file/search); on match dispatches forgeos:jump-to-file event; EditTab listens, switches cockpit to Edit tab, loads file, scrolls to line, selects matched text, brief blue flash confirms landing
searchWorkspaceFiles(runId, searchText) added to server/workspace/manager.js — searches text files, returns up to 10 {file, line, snippet} matches
GET /api/runs/:id/file/search?text=... API route added to server/index.js
Upgraded all Render services to Standard tier
GitHub PAT rotated — new PAT stored in context pack, not chat
Updated ForgeOS.md — Visual Editor phases, version history, custom domains, pipeline cleanup documented
decisions: Visual editor ships in three phases: Phase 2 (Edit tab) → Phase 1 (Inspect Mode) → Phase 3 (Inspect-to-Edit bridge)
Inspector script injected dormant into every preview HTML response — activated/deactivated via postMessage
Phase 3 falls back gracefully to Phase 1 behavior when no file match found (dynamic/JS-rendered content)
All Render services upgraded to Standard tier — Starter tier fully retired
GitHub PAT stored in context pack only — never in chat history
server/pipeline/ now contains only runner.js — agents.js and schemas.js deleted

session_log.session_title: Cockpit UI Polish — Diff Tab, Collapsible Chat, Mobile Scroll Fix
shipped: Diff tab — side-by-side code diff between any two iterations; two version selectors (A=red, B=green) defaulting to previous vs current; LCS-based line diff with aligned blank rows; +/− badges for new/deleted files; 'No changes' message when identical
Collapsible chat column — chatCollapsed state in App.tsx; ‹/› toggle tab on right edge of prompt column; smooth width transition (0.2s); gives workspace full width when collapsed; hidden on mobile
Mobile chat scroll fix — .mobile-panel-chat .prompt-column gets min-height: 0, height: 100%, overflow: hidden so .chat-thread can scroll properly
decisions: Diff tab uses LCS algorithm for line-level diffing — side-by-side columns with aligned blank rows for readability
Chat collapse hidden on mobile — mobile already uses panel switching, collapse would conflict
Collapsible chat uses CSS width transition rather than DOM removal — preserves state when re-expanded

session_log.session_title: Iteration Rollbacks, deleteIteration Endpoint, Plan Gate & Surgical Build System
shipped: Manual branch rollbacks for sandbox-xm — force-pushed apps/sandbox-xm branch to 6152e5ef5e5a (18:51 UTC March 10, last clean build before Resend damage) via GitHub API

DELETE /api/projects/:id/runs/:runId endpoint — added deleteIteration() to server/projects/manager.js; removes iteration from iterations table, deletes run snapshot, rolls currentRunId back to previous iteration automatically; route added to server/index.js

Pre-build plan approval gate — server/plan/manager.js: generatePlan() (haiku model) produces structured JSON {taskSummary, approach, filesToCreate, filesToModify, filesOffLimits}; planToConstraintBlock() serializes approved plan into hard constraint block prepended to builder system prompt
POST /api/projects/:id/plan route in server/index.js
/iterate now accepts optional approvedPlan in request body — threaded through createRun → buildAndDeploy → buildWorkspace
PlanTab rewritten in Workspace.tsx — three states: loading spinner, pending approval gate (Approve & Build / Revise Prompt), post-build summary; file badges color-coded green(+)/yellow(~)/red(✗)
Plan gate CSS added to index.css — .plan-gate-*, .plan-approve-btn, .plan-revise-btn, .plan-loading-spinner, .plan-badge-*, .plan-file-*
iterateProject() updated in App.tsx — accepts optional { skipPlan } flag; skipPlan: true bypasses plan gate and fires /iterate directly (used for Chat Agent build suggestions)
onRunBuild prop type updated in PromptColumn.tsx — accepts optional { skipPlan?: boolean }
handleBuildFromSuggestion updated — calls onRunBuild(suggestion, { skipPlan: true }) so Chat Agent suggestions bypass the plan gate
isSuggestion flag threaded end-to-end — client sends isSuggestion: true in /iterate body for suggestion builds; server/index.js passes to createRun; runner.js stores on run object

suggestionToConstraintBlock() added to server/plan/manager.js — parses target file from Chat Agent suggestion text using regex /\bin\s+([\w./-]+\.\w+)/i; builds surgical single-file constraint block; returns { constraintBlock, targetFile }; safe fallback locks ALL files when regex fails
buildWorkspace() updated — accepts isSuggestion param; when true calls suggestionToConstraintBlock, stores _suggestionTarget on output, caller stores as run.suggestionTarget

Plan tab surgical build visibility — when isSuggestion build is running, Plan tab shows 'Surgical edit in progress' with target file in green monospace; shows yellow warning if regex fallback triggered; CSS added: .plan-surgical-status, .plan-surgical-label, .plan-surgical-target, .plan-surgical-file, .plan-surgical-fallback
decisions: Safe failure over destructive success — if suggestionToConstraintBlock cannot parse the target file, all files go off-limits rather than allowing an unconstrained build
Plan gate fires for all raw chat prompts; Chat Agent build suggestions bypass the gate via skipPlan: true + isSuggestion: true
Inspect mode 'Use in chat' workflow preserved — Chat Agent still diagnoses and writes buildSuggestion; only the downstream build is now surgical
deleteIteration rolls currentRunId back automatically to previous iteration — no manual DB update needed after deleting a bad iteration

Iteration rollback via GitHub API force-push to apps/<slug> branch — does not create a new commit, just moves branch pointer to existing tag commit; Render may not auto-trigger on force-push, manual redeploy may be needed
suggestionToConstraintBlock returns object { constraintBlock, targetFile } not a plain string — caller must destructure; _suggestionTarget deleted from builderOutput after being stored on run
pending: Point sandbox-xm.com DNS fully — A record @ → Render IP at Namecheap + www URL redirect
Build Restore Iteration UI button in iterations dropdown — server-side rollbackToVersion exists for published apps; need workspace equivalent
open_questions: Should Render redeploy be triggered automatically after a force-push rollback, or always require manual trigger?
Should the Plan tab show the full off-limits file list during a surgical build, or just the target file?

session_log.session_title: Surgical Build Fixes — Workspace Seeding, Missing Entry File Guard, RunData Types
shipped: Fixed TypeScript build failure — added isSuggestion?: boolean and suggestionTarget?: string | null to RunData interface in client/src/App.tsx; Render build was failing with TS2339 errors

Fixed surgical build deleting server.js — createWorkspace() wipes the entire workspace directory on every run; surgical builds only return the target file so all other files were deleted; fix: when isSuggestion is true, write existingFiles into the fresh workspace before writing builder output, so only the target file changes

Fixed no-op surgical build restart loop — when fallback locks all files, builder writes nothing, server.js missing, startApp crashed in restart loop; fix: added entry file existence check in startApp() before spawning process; sets status to start-failed with clear error message instead of looping
Deleted sandbox-xm v25 (runId 7f9b4cfe) via DELETE /api/projects/f9bc3895/runs/7f9b4cfe — project rolled back to v24 (f39bf14c) automatically
Identified and rejected Forge's incorrect self-diagnosis — Forge suggested regenerating server.js on every HTML edit; correct fix was workspace seeding, not a workaround
decisions: Surgical builds seed workspace from existingFiles before writing builder output — preserves all untouched files, only target file changes
startApp entry file guard: node <file> commands check existsSync before spawn — fast fail with clear error, no restart loop
deleteIteration can be called directly via Neon DB query + HMAC cookie generation from local .env — no need to log into ForgeOS UI to delete bad iterations

Forge's self-diagnosis on surgical build failures should not be trusted — it misidentified the root cause as 'executor not creating server.js' when the real issue was workspace wipe on createWorkspace
pending: Point sandbox-xm.com DNS fully — A record @ → Render IP at Namecheap + www URL redirect
Build Restore Iteration UI button in iterations dropdown
Test Stage 2 React/Vite build end-to-end — verify Mode B scaffolds vite.config.js correctly on first pass
open_questions: Should Render redeploy be triggered automatically after a force-push rollback, or always require manual trigger?
Should the Plan tab show the full off-limits file list during a surgical build, or just the target file?

session_log.session_title: Restore Iteration Feature — Implementation, Three Bug Fixes, sandbox-xm v33/v34 Cleanup
shipped: Implemented restoreIteration(projectId, runId) in server/projects/manager.js — loads snapshot from Neon run_snapshots, stops current project app (NOT all apps), seeds fresh workspace from snapshot files, installs deps, starts app, updates project.currentRunId in memory and DB
POST /api/projects/:id/restore/:runId endpoint added to server/index.js
Added loadAndRegisterRun(runId) to server/pipeline/runner.js — checks if run already in Map, loads snapshot from DB, registers in runs Map so UI can poll post-restore; exported from runner.js

restoreIteration callback wired in client/src/App.tsx — POST /api/projects/${currentProjectId}/restore/${runId}, sets currentRunId and clears viewingIterationRunId on success; passed as onRestoreIteration prop to PromptColumn
'Restore this version' button added to viewing-history-banner in client/src/components/PromptColumn.tsx — calls onRestoreIteration(viewingIterationRunId) on click
Fixed bug 1: stopAllApps() killing all projects' workspaces during restore — changed to workspace.stopApp(project.currentRunId), only stops current project
Fixed bug 2: no guard for missing snapshots — restoreIteration now fails fast with clear error before any filesystem changes if snapshot not found in DB
Fixed bug 3: restored run not in runner's runs Map — loadAndRegisterRun() called after workspace recreation to put past snapshot back in Map so polling works
Deleted sandbox-xm v33 (run had no snapshot, was built on broken state) and v34 (also no snapshot) via deleteIteration — project rolled back to v32 (0fb0c8d1)
Confirmed v32 (0fb0c8d1) has valid snapshot with 3 files at stages.builder.output.files — verified via direct Neon query
decisions: restoreIteration must call stopApp(currentRunId) not stopAllApps() — stopAllApps destroys every project's workspace across the board, catastrophic in multi-project environments
Snapshot guard must fail before any filesystem changes — if no snapshot exists, function returns error immediately; workspace is never touched
loadAndRegisterRun required after restore — runner's in-memory Map is the source of truth for UI polling; a workspace with no Map entry leaves the UI unable to display the run
Runs with no snapshot (v33, v34) cannot be restored — they were built on broken state and snapshot was never saved; delete these with deleteIteration
pending: Test Restore Iteration feature end-to-end now that three bugs are fixed
Point sandbox-xm.com DNS fully — A record @ → Render IP at Namecheap + www URL redirect
Test Stage 2 React/Vite build end-to-end — verify Mode B scaffolds vite.config.js correctly on first pass
Consider pgvector semantic search upgrade for Brain
open_questions: Should Render redeploy be triggered automatically after a force-push rollback, or always require manual trigger?
Should the Plan tab show the full off-limits file list during a surgical build, or just the target file?

domain_knowledge: ForgeOS serves workspace previews at /preview/:runId/ and published apps via *.forge-os.ai subdomains behind a path-prefix proxy
All Claude-generated workspace apps must use root-relative URLs with a leading slash (e.g. /api/items, /style.css) — the proxy handles prefix rewriting, apps must never do it themselves
Never add <base> tags to any app — they break proxy rewriting
Preview proxy (/preview/:runId) intercepts ALL requests including /api/assets/* — fix applied in server/index.js short-circuits asset requests to ForgeOS root handler
Generated apps must fetch global assets client-side using /api/assets/FILENAME — never server-side via localhost self-call
ForgeOS apps run in two modes: Mode A (node server.js, plain Node, default) or Mode B (npm run dev, Vite+React, opt-in)
Mode A: CommonJS only, no bundlers, no build steps, plain HTML/CSS/JS frontend
Mode B: Vite + React or Vue only — vite.config.js required, server.port must bind to process.env.PORT, server.host: true
The platform injects environment variables at runtime — no dotenv, no .env files
PORT must always be process.env.PORT || 3000 — never hardcoded
Every Mode A app requires GET / returning a complete HTML page — not a redirect, not JSON
Database: @neondatabase/serverless only, all CREATE TABLE statements use IF NOT EXISTS, FK column types must exactly match referenced PK types
Authentication: Neon Auth via jose + JWKS only — no bcrypt, no custom JWT signing, no passport
Claude frequently wraps JSON responses in markdown fences — always strip before JSON.parse
ForgeOS Brain (server/memory/brain.js) provides persistent memory across all projects and team members via Neon Postgres
Brain memory is non-blocking — extractMemory is called after successful builds but never blocks the build response
The Chat Agent degradation pattern: after failed iterations it loses confidence and reaches for logging/try-catch — this is a named failure mode, not acceptable behavior
Logging is never a fix. try-catch is never a fix. Find the actual broken code.
buildSuggestion must contain: file + route/function + current broken code + replacement. One sentence. No outcome descriptions.
Never write inline backtick-wrapped content (e.g. code examples) inside JS template literal strings — Node parser treats backtick as closing the template literal
Claude Code PRs (claude/* branches) must be reviewed before merge — they can introduce syntax errors and conflicting prompt changes
The Visual Editor has three phases: Phase 1 (Inspect Mode in Render tab), Phase 2 (Edit tab with split-pane file editor), Phase 3 (Inspect-to-Edit bridge via background file search)
Inspector script is injected dormant into every preview HTML page via rewriteHtmlForProxy — activated via postMessage forge:inspect:activate, deactivated via forge:inspect:deactivate
Phase 3 file search uses GET /api/runs/:id/file/search?text=... — searches text files, returns up to 10 {file, line, snippet} matches
forgeos:jump-to-file custom event carries {file, line} — EditTab listens and jumps to file+line with blue flash confirmation
server/pipeline/ contains only runner.js — agents.js and schemas.js were deleted in the March 10 cleanup
Plan gate: raw chat prompts go through generatePlan() (haiku) → user approves → /iterate with approvedPlan → planToConstraintBlock prepended to builder system prompt

Surgical builds: Chat Agent buildSuggestion clicks bypass plan gate via isSuggestion: true → suggestionToConstraintBlock parses target file from suggestion text → single-file constraint block prepended to builder prompt
suggestionToConstraintBlock safe fallback: if regex /\bin\s+([\w./-]+\.\w+)/i cannot parse a file path, ALL existing files go off-limits — builder cannot touch anything, safe failure mode
run.suggestionTarget stores the parsed target file name — surfaced in Plan tab as 'Surgical edit in progress / Targeting: <file>'
deleteIteration(projectId, runId) in server/projects/manager.js — deletes from iterations table + run_snapshots, removes from in-memory array, auto-rolls currentRunId back to previous iteration

Iteration rollback pattern: force-push apps/<slug> branch to target tag's commit SHA via GitHub API PATCH /repos/:owner/:repo/git/refs/heads/apps/:slug — does not create new commit, Render may need manual redeploy trigger

Surgical build workspace seeding: createWorkspace() deletes entire workspace dir on every run; for isSuggestion builds, write existingFiles first then overwrite with builder output — only the target file changes, all other files survive
startApp entry file guard: before spawning node <file>, check fs.existsSync — if missing, set status=start-failed and return immediately instead of crashing in restart loop

deleteIteration via local env: query Neon with NEON_DATABASE_URL to get runId, generate HMAC cookie from SESSION_SECRET using gate.js signToken logic, call DELETE /api/projects/:id/runs/:runId with Cookie: fos_session=<token>
run_snapshots Neon table stores run data as JSONB; files are at stages.builder.output.files (array of {path, content} objects) — not at top-level or builder.files
restoreIteration requires loadAndRegisterRun after workspace recreation — runner.js runs Map is the source of truth for UI polling; skipping this leaves the UI unable to see the restored run
stopApp(currentRunId) vs stopAllApps() — always use the scoped form in project-specific operations; stopAllApps kills every project's workspace process and is only appropriate for shutdown
Runs with no snapshot in run_snapshots cannot be restored — if snapshot is missing, restoreIteration fails fast before touching filesystem; delete these iterations via deleteIteration instead

decisions_made.decision: Replace 11-prompt multi-agent pipeline with single Claude workspace builder prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Pipeline had too many inter-agent communication failure points. Single model with full context reasons through the whole build in one pass.

decisions_made.decision: Assets are global scope — no project_id
decisions_made.rationale: Project-scoped assets fell out on reforge. Global assets persist across all projects and rebuilds.

decisions_made.decision: Stage 2 tech stack unbans: React/Vue via Vite
decisions_made.rationale: CDN-based React already worked. npm-based React via Vite opens full component ecosystem with controlled bundler choice.

decisions_made.decision: Mode A / Mode B runtime structure in builder prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Without explicit modes the builder would guess when to use Vite. Mode A is default, Mode B is opt-in — clear decision tree prevents unnecessary framework use.

decisions_made.decision: All Anthropic API calls use claude-opus-4-5
decisions_made.rationale: Most capable model, chosen for quality over cost given the stakes of first-pass build quality.

decisions_made.decision: Memory extraction uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 not opus
decisions_made.rationale: Memory extraction is a lightweight classification task — haiku is fast and cheap.

decisions_made.decision: ForgeOS Brain stores memories in Neon Postgres, not a separate vector DB
decisions_made.rationale: Neon supports pgvector natively — no additional infrastructure needed.

decisions_made.decision: Replit sunset — VS Code is sole dev environment
decisions_made.rationale: Replit caused workflow friction. VS Code + GitHub + Render is cleaner and more reliable.

decisions_made.decision: Visual editor ships Phase 2 → Phase 1 → Phase 3
decisions_made.rationale: Phase 2 (direct file edit) delivers immediate value with zero pipeline risk. Phase 1 (inspect) adds element targeting. Phase 3 (bridge) closes the loop — each phase is useful standalone.

decisions_made.decision: Inspector script injected dormant into all preview HTML, activated via postMessage
decisions_made.rationale: No server round-trip needed to toggle inspect mode. Keeps the iframe self-contained and the activation instantaneous.

decisions_made.decision: All Render services upgraded to Standard tier — Starter retired
decisions_made.rationale: Always-on requirement. Starter tier spins down after inactivity.

decisions_made.decision: GitHub PAT stored in context pack only, never in chat
decisions_made.rationale: Chat history is less controlled. Context pack is attached intentionally per session.

decisions_made.decision: Safe failure over destructive success for surgical builds
decisions_made.rationale: If suggestionToConstraintBlock cannot parse the target file, all files go off-limits. A build that does nothing is recoverable in seconds; a build that rewrites wrong files can take an hour to unwind.

decisions_made.decision: Plan gate bypassed for Chat Agent build suggestions (isSuggestion: true)
decisions_made.rationale: Chat Agent already scopes the change to one file and one fix. Running the planner on top of that is redundant and causes the planner to treat the suggestion as a feature request, generating overly broad multi-file plans.

decisions_made.decision: deleteIteration auto-rolls currentRunId to previous iteration
decisions_made.rationale: Manual DB correction after deletion is error-prone. Rolling back automatically keeps the project state consistent without extra steps.
stopAllApps() is a global shutdown tool — calling it during a single-project restore destroys every other running workspace. Scoped stopApp() is the correct primitive for project-level operations.

decisions_made.decision: restoreIteration fails fast on missing snapshot before any filesystem changes
decisions_made.rationale: If the snapshot doesn't exist, there's nothing to restore. Any filesystem changes before the guard would leave the workspace in a destroyed state with no valid code to run.

decisions_made.decision: loadAndRegisterRun() added to runner.js to put past snapshots back in the runs Map
decisions_made.rationale: The UI polls the runs Map directly for status and file data. A workspace that's running but not in the Map is invisible to the user — they see a spinner forever.

preferences.value: Never use 'refactor' — the correct term is 'reforge'
CommonJS (require/module.exports) for server — ES modules allowed in Vite/React frontend (Mode B only)
Plain HTML/CSS/JS (Mode A) — Vite+React (Mode B) only when explicitly requested
claude-opus-4-5 for all primary builds and reasoning tasks
Conversational — expect iteration, build on what works, don't rebuild from scratch
Direct, no hedging, no try-catch suggestions, no logging suggestions
Docx files for reference documents, .md for prompts and integration guides
Use 'I'll make this update' for changes, improvements, polish, and feature additions. Reserve 'I found the bug' for actual crashes, broken behavior, or runtime errors only.
Safe failure always preferred over destructive success — a build that does nothing is better than one that damages files

patterns_that_worked: Single Claude call with full platform constraints replaces multi-agent pipeline — simpler, fewer failure points
Memory extraction as a non-blocking post-build call — never blocks user-facing response
buildContext() prepended to system prompt as a separate block — clean separation of memory from instructions
Per-project conversation history stored in DB and passed as messages array — gives Claude full build journey awareness
Pre-emit self-checklist in builder prompt — model gates itself before output
Fence-strip + first-brace-scan pattern for all Claude JSON responses — handles all three Claude output styles
Preview proxy /api/assets/* passthrough — short-circuit to ForgeOS root handler, never forward to workspace app
Mode A / Mode B explicit runtime modes — prevents builder from guessing when to use Vite
Full syntax check (node --check) across all server JS files before every push
Three-phase visual editor — each phase useful standalone, phases compose into a full inspect-to-edit workflow
Dormant inspector script injected into all preview HTML — zero latency activation via postMessage
forgeos:jump-to-file custom event decouples RenderTab from EditTab — no prop threading needed
Plan gate constraint block prepended to builder system prompt — gives builder explicit permission and denial lists before it touches any file
suggestionToConstraintBlock safe fallback — locks all files when target cannot be parsed, never allows unconstrained build
isSuggestion flag threaded end-to-end (client → request body → createRun → run object → buildWorkspace) — clean signal for surgical build path with no ambiguity
Iteration rollback via GitHub API force-push — fast, no new commits, clean branch history
mistakes_to_avoid: Inline backtick-wrapped content inside JS template literal strings — Node parser treats backtick as closing delimiter
Merging Claude Code PRs (claude/* branches) without review — PR #33 introduced syntax errors in builder.js and agents.js
Chat Agent suggesting logging or try-catch after failed iterations — named as degradation pattern, explicitly banned
Generated apps fetching assets server-side via localhost — always fetch client-side using root-relative /api/assets/FILENAME
getAssetsContext() returning array — must return formatted string for filter(Boolean).join() to work in builder.js
Fetch URL audit rule inverted — correct rule is: fetch argument must start with / and must not contain ://
Spreading process.env wholesale into child processes — host secrets bleed into published apps
URL helper functions (apiUrl(), buildUrl()) that construct absolute URLs — fix fetch() calls directly with /path strings
Multiple code changes in a single buildSuggestion — one root cause, one code change
Format rules at top of long prompts — model drops them under context pressure
Using execSync with shell string interpolation for paths — use execFileSync with args array

Running the plan gate on Chat Agent build suggestions — planner treats the suggestion as a feature request and generates a broad multi-file plan, which then gives the builder permission to touch files it should never touch
Force-push rollback without triggering Render redeploy — Render webhook may not fire on force-push since no new commit SHA is created; always verify the live site reflects the rollback

Trusting Forge's self-diagnosis on surgical build failures — Forge misidentified the root cause as 'executor not creating server.js' and proposed regenerating server.js on every HTML edit; real issue was createWorkspace() wiping the workspace; always trace the actual code path
Surgical builds writing only builder output without seeding existingFiles — createWorkspace wipes the dir, builder returns only the target file, all other files deleted
Using stopAllApps() in restoreIteration — kills every running workspace across all projects; always use stopApp(currentRunId) for project-scoped operations
Skipping loadAndRegisterRun after restoreIteration — workspace runs but UI cannot see it; runner Map must be updated after any workspace recreation

Attempting to restore a run with no snapshot — restoreIteration will fail; verify snapshot exists at stages.builder.output.files in run_snapshots before expecting restore to work; delete no-snapshot runs with deleteIteration instead
current_state: ForgeOS is fully operational on Render. The single-prompt builder pipeline is live with Brain memory injection, global assets injection, and Mode A/Mode B runtime support. The Visual Editor is complete across all three phases. The cockpit includes a Diff tab, collapsible chat column, and mobile scroll fix. Version history with git tags and rollback is live. BYO custom domain support is live. The pre-build plan approval gate is live. Surgical build system is live and confirmed working — surgical builds seed existingFiles before writing builder output, startApp has an entry file guard against restart loops. Restore Iteration feature is live (POST /api/projects/:id/restore/:runId) with three bugs fixed: stopAllApps replaced with scoped stopApp, snapshot guard before filesystem changes, and loadAndRegisterRun registration after restore. deleteIteration is live and operational. sandbox-xm project_id is f9bc3895, currently on v32 (0fb0c8d1) after v25/v33/v34 were deleted. Run v32 has confirmed valid snapshot with 3 files at stages.builder.output.files.
next_steps: Test Restore Iteration feature end-to-end — bugs fixed, needs validation in live environment
Point sandbox-xm.com DNS fully — A record @ → Render IP at Namecheap + www URL redirect
Test Stage 2 React/Vite build end-to-end — verify Mode B scaffolds vite.config.js correctly on first pass
Consider pgvector semantic search upgrade once Brain has sufficient memory entries
open_questions: Should Render redeploy be triggered automatically after a force-push rollback, or always require manual trigger?
Should the Plan tab show the full off-limits file list during a surgical build, or just the target file?
Should failed builds auto-record to Brain or require human confirmation?
What is the right conversation history limit per project before summarization is needed?
Should workspace builder stream responses or return complete JSON on completion?

key_terminology.value: The correct term for what other systems call 'refactor' — we reforge code, never refactor it
The AI-powered web app builder cockpit, deployed on Render at forge-os.ai
A directory containing files for a single generated app, identified by runId
The persistent memory module (server/memory/brain.js) storing patterns, mistakes, preferences and project history across all ForgeOS builds
The ForgeOS UI — tab strip (Plan/Review/Diff/Auditor/Render/Edit/Shell/DB/Env/Publish) plus global Assets sidebar and collapsible chat column
Plain Node runtime — node server.js, CommonJS, no bundlers, plain HTML/CSS/JS frontend. Default mode.
Vite+React runtime — npm run dev, Vite bundler, React or Vue frontend. Opt-in only.
Files uploaded to forge_assets table in Neon, accessible at /api/assets/:filename by any project
The ForgeOS middleware that serves apps at /preview/:runId/ and rewrites HTML URLs to stay within the proxy path
A structured JSON export of project knowledge in a model-agnostic format for portability between AI systems
The Chat Agent failure mode where after failed iterations it suggests logging or try-catch instead of finding the actual broken code
The ForgeOS settings feature that auto-injects API keys and secrets as environment variables at runtime
Three-phase inspect-to-edit system: Phase 1 (Inspect Mode), Phase 2 (Edit tab), Phase 3 (Inspect-to-Edit bridge)
Custom window event carrying {file, line} — dispatched by RenderTab after a successful file search, consumed by EditTab to jump to the matched location
The pre-build approval step where generatePlan() produces a structured plan for user review before /iterate fires. Only triggers for raw chat prompts, not suggestion builds.

A build triggered by a Chat Agent buildSuggestion. Bypasses the plan gate. Uses suggestionToConstraintBlock to lock all files except the parsed target. Identified by isSuggestion: true on the run object.

Function in server/plan/manager.js that parses the target file from a Chat Agent suggestion using regex, builds a single-file constraint block, and returns { constraintBlock, targetFile }. Safe fallback locks all files if regex fails.

Function in server/projects/manager.js and endpoint DELETE /api/projects/:id/runs/:runId that removes an iteration from DB, deletes the run snapshot, and auto-rolls currentRunId back to the previous iteration.

Function in server/projects/manager.js and endpoint POST /api/projects/:id/restore/:runId. Loads snapshot from Neon, stops current project app, recreates workspace, installs deps, starts app, calls loadAndRegisterRun, updates project.currentRunId. Fails fast with error if snapshot missing.

Function in server/pipeline/runner.js. Loads a past run snapshot from Neon and registers it in the in-memory runs Map. Required after restoreIteration so the UI can poll the restored run's status and files.
